title: Corytoplectus riceanus
taxonomy: Corytoplectus riceanus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is commonly known as the African violet family. The genus Corytoplectus is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Corytoplectus riceanus is native to Cameroon, a country in central Africa. The classification of Corytoplectus riceanus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Gesneriaceae family.

intro: Corytoplectus riceanus, also known as riceanus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Corytoplectus riceanus include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects.
